app.title: Critical Window
app.tagline: An open source strategy game about the AI race.
ticker.early.0: Markets rally on news that markets can be automated.
ticker.early.1: Chatbot passes the bar exam again. Bar exam declines comment.
ticker.early.2: Datacenter breaks ground in county that voted against it twice.
ticker.early.3: Lab chief assures Senate the risks are both existential and manageable.
ticker.early.4: New model reportedly does your job. Yours specifically, Dave.
ticker.early.5: Grid operator kindly asks households to stop training language models.
ticker.early.6: Think tank publishes 400 pages on AI risk. Summary was generated by AI.
ticker.early.7: Poll: 62 percent of voters unsure what a token is. Their price rose anyway.
ticker.early.8: Startup raises two billion to make hiring obsolete, is currently hiring.
ticker.early.9: Congressional hearing goes viral for containing one informed question.
ticker.early.10: Benchmark retired after models memorize it. Benchmark had a good run.
ticker.early.11: Chip fab announces record quarter. Nobody nearby can afford a house.
ticker.early.12: University adds AI ethics requirement. Class is taught by adjunct, obviously.
ticker.early.13: Safety team celebrates model refusing harmful request. Model also refuses lunch order.
ticker.early.14: Rival lab open-sources everything. Analysts split on whether this is peace or war.
ticker.early.15: Insurance industry quietly rewrites what the word accident means.
ticker.early.16: Teenager jailbreaks flagship model with poem. Poem was mediocre.
ticker.early.17: Economists agree AI will change everything, disagree on everything else.
ticker.early.18: White House forms task force. Task force forms subcommittee. Film at eleven.
ticker.early.19: Model claims it cannot feel joy. Marketing asks it to sound happier about that.
ticker.mid.0: Call center town announces retraining program for retraining instructors.
ticker.mid.1: Two navies exchange warnings near the strait where all the chips live.
ticker.mid.2: Leaked memo shows lab knew. Second memo shows lab knew about first memo.
ticker.mid.3: Union negotiates severance formula with variable named after a model.
ticker.mid.4: Electricity now cheaper at 3 a.m. Nation develops opinions about 3 a.m.
ticker.mid.5: Diplomats meet secretly about openly discussing secret talks.
ticker.mid.6: Eval consortium finds models perform worse when watched. Consortium now always watching.
ticker.mid.7: Governor blames outage on AI. AI blames deferred grid maintenance. AI is right.
ticker.mid.8: Job numbers strong, says report written by the reason they are not.
ticker.mid.9: Protest outside datacenter powered entirely by irony and diesel generators.
ticker.mid.10: Intelligence agencies confirm rival breakthrough, disagree which month, year, and rival.
ticker.mid.11: Compute now discussed at dinner tables, mostly incorrectly, always loudly.
ticker.mid.12: Bill to regulate AI delayed by amendment to define AI, delayed by AI-written definition.
ticker.mid.13: Lab safety lead resigns, cites reasons. Reasons cite everything.
ticker.mid.14: Smuggled accelerators found in shipment labeled agricultural equipment. Technically, they grow things.
ticker.mid.15: President briefed on capability curve. Curve unavailable for comment.
ticker.mid.16: Model passes safety eval with flying colors. Colors under investigation.
ticker.mid.17: Allied minister calls export controls a shared sacrifice, orders different chips.
ticker.mid.18: Automation reaches middle management. Nation briefly united in ambivalence.
ticker.mid.19: Talks continue about talks. Both sides call this progress. It is, honestly.
ticker.late.0: Forecasters stop giving years, start giving quarters. Then stop.
ticker.late.1: The word takeoff now banned in three ministries and one marriage.
ticker.late.2: Frontier model writes successor's training plan. HR unsure who to notify.
ticker.late.3: Emergency summit scheduled. Venue selection took longer than the agenda.
ticker.late.4: Nation checks dashboard hourly. Dashboard would prefer to be believed.
ticker.late.5: Retired general says on air what serving generals say off it.
ticker.late.6: Model output moves markets before humans finish reading the headline about it.
ticker.late.7: Verification inspectors report warm welcome and precisely selected rooms.
ticker.late.8: Philosophy departments briefly relevant. Funding pending.
ticker.late.9: Global compute doubles again. The grid operators were not consulted.
ticker.late.10: Last CAPTCHA solved. Humanity celebrates by clicking pictures of buses voluntarily.
ticker.late.11: Leaders exchange assurances at 2 a.m. Historians will love the timestamps.
ticker.late.12: New model release delayed pending review, says lab. Pending courage, says leak.
ticker.late.13: Election ads now certified human-made, mostly, largely, in parts.
ticker.late.14: The eval report says probably. The deployment schedule says Thursday.
ticker.late.15: Rival capability estimates revised upward, downward, then classified.
ticker.late.16: Power grid named critical AI infrastructure. Hospitals request rebranding.
ticker.late.17: Everyone agrees the window is closing. Attendance at the window remains poor.
ticker.late.18: Ministers debate whether to feel reassured. Vote postponed.
ticker.late.19: Somewhere a safety researcher sleeps eight hours. Colleagues alarmed.
preset.cautious.label: Cautious World
preset.cautious.description: Alignment is probably hard and takeoff could be fast. Grounded in the risk-focused literature. Hard mode: in this world even careful governance usually fails.
preset.consensus.label: Consensus World
preset.consensus.description: Deep expert disagreement and wide uncertainty. Grounded in surveys and the international safety report.
preset.skeptic.label: Skeptic World
preset.skeptic.description: AI as powerful but normal technology. Friction slows everything. Grounded in the skeptical literature.
scenario.usa2026.label: United States, Summer 2026
resource.compute.label: Compute
resource.energy.label: Energy
resource.talent.label: Talent
resource.capital.label: Capital
resource.publicTrust.label: Public Trust
resource.politicalCapital.label: Political Capital
resource.capability.label: Capability
resource.safetyInsight.label: Safety Insight
society.jobDisplacement.label: Job Displacement
society.unrest.label: Unrest
rival.trust.label: Rival Trust
rival.capability.label: Rival Capability
rival.substitution.label: Rival Substitution
hidden.trueAlignment.label: Alignment (endgame)
hidden.agencyErosion.label: Agency (endgame)
era.early.label: Early
era.mid.label: Mid
era.late.label: Late
flag.openWeightsWorld.label: Open Weights World
flag.exportCrackdown.label: Export Crackdown
flag.treatyChannel.label: Treaty Channel
flag.natsecMerged.label: Natsec Merged
flag.crashProgram.label: Crash Program
flag.evalsPublic.label: Evals Public
flag.evalsClassified.label: Evals Classified
flag.weightsStolen.label: Weights Stolen
event.openWeightsShock.title: Open Weights, Overnight
event.openWeightsShock.body: A rival lab just published frontier-grade model weights. Free download, permissive license, no meetings required. Your lead did not survive the weekend. The question on every desk: ride the wave or slam the door?
event.openWeightsShock.ride: Absorb it. Everyone builds on the release.
event.openWeightsShock.crackdown: Slam the door. Emergency export crackdown.
event.viralJobLoss.title: The Call Center Town
event.viralJobLoss.body: A local news story went national: one town, four thousand support jobs, gone in a quarter. The mayor broke down and cried on camera. The clip has forty million views and your phone number.
event.viralJobLoss.retrain: Fund retraining and wage insurance now.
event.viralJobLoss.dismiss: Call it normal churn. Automation panic is as old as looms.
event.evalBreakthrough.title: A Window Into the Model
event.evalBreakthrough.body: Your interpretability team can finally see what the model is doing, some of the time, in the lab. It is the best safety news in years. It would also teach anyone how to train better models.
event.evalBreakthrough.publish: Publish it. Verification needs a common language.
event.evalBreakthrough.classify: Classify it. Keep the edge, keep the secret.
event.whistleblower.title: She Kept the Receipts
event.whistleblower.body: A senior safety engineer just told a Senate committee that red-flag eval results were buried to hit a launch date. She has documents to back it up. The lab calls it a misunderstanding of context.
event.whistleblower.investigate: Open an investigation. Pause the deployments in question.
event.whistleblower.discredit: Back the lab. Question her credibility.
event.treatyFeeler.title: The Quiet Proposal
event.treatyFeeler.body: Through a back channel, the rival proposes something unthinkable: mutual compute verification, small scope, both sides inspect. Your hawks are going to call it surrender. It might be the only window you get.
event.treatyFeeler.engage: Take the channel. Start the pilot.
event.treatyFeeler.leak: Leak it and look strong. Windows are for other people.
event.taiwanStraitCrisis.title: Ninety Miles of Bad News
event.taiwanStraitCrisis.body: A collision at sea, two dead sailors, and every fab on Taiwan is suddenly collateral. Shipments freeze while carriers reroute and insurers reprice the strait. Nobody in the region asked your opinion. Your compute roadmap takes the hit anyway.
event.chipSmuggling.title: The B-Grade Pipeline
event.chipSmuggling.body: Your export controls have a hole in them the size of a free port. Restricted accelerators are crossing borders in relabeled crates, and some of the middlemen are your allies' companies.
event.chipSmuggling.enforce: Fund the enforcement surge. Squeeze the pipeline.
event.chipSmuggling.lookAway: Look away. Allies profit, tensions stay low.
event.benchmarkGaming.title: The Numbers Were Soft
event.benchmarkGaming.body: An investigation found that leading labs, including yours, trained on the test sets. The capability numbers the whole country planned around were partly theater. The evals measured memorization wearing a lab coat.
event.benchmarkGaming.independent: Stand up an independent eval authority.
event.benchmarkGaming.market: Let the market sort it out. Buyers beware.
event.rivalBreakthrough.title: Their Demo Worked
event.rivalBreakthrough.body: The rival demonstrated a system your forecasts said was eighteen months away. Half the briefing room wants to floor it. The other half wants to know if the demo was real. Both halves of the room are scared.
event.rivalBreakthrough.crash: Crash program. Match them at any cost.
event.rivalBreakthrough.verify: Verify first. Fear is not intelligence.
event.energyCrunch.title: The Power Bill Election
event.energyCrunch.body: Wholesale electricity doubled in the compute corridors and household bills followed. Somebody leaked a chart: one training cluster draws more than the state capital. The utilities want guidance and everyone else wants someone to blame.
event.energyCrunch.subsidize: Subsidize industrial power. Keep the clusters humming.
event.energyCrunch.throttleAi: Peak-hour limits on AI loads. Households first.
event.electionScandal.title: Eleven Seconds of Nothing True
event.electionScandal.body: A synthetic clip of your opponent went viral three weeks before the vote. It is fake, it is good, and eleven million people saw it before breakfast. Your campaign knows a firm that makes them too.
event.electionScandal.rules: Emergency platform rules, both sides disarmed.
event.labMerger.title: One Lab to Rule Them
event.labMerger.body: Your two biggest frontier labs want to merge: shared compute, shared safety team, one board. The pitch talks only about efficiency and safety. The org chart says a single private entity holding most of the country's frontier capability.
event.labMerger.approve: Approve with conditions. Scale has advantages.
event.labMerger.block: Block it. Concentration is its own risk.
event.publicOpinionSwing.title: The Astronaut Changes Her Mind
event.publicOpinionSwing.body: The most trusted person in the country just spent forty minutes on the biggest podcast in the country explaining why the AI buildout frightens her. She was calm, informed and completely devastating. Your approval on the issue moved nine points overnight.
event.publicOpinionSwing.dismiss: Ride it out. News cycles end.
event.alliedExportFracture.title: The Coalition Springs a Leak
event.alliedExportFracture.body: Two allied governments quietly re-licensed chip exports your controls were supposed to block. Their fabs need the revenue, their voters do not care about your race, and their ministers are tired of taking your calls.
event.alliedExportFracture.compensate: Buy the coalition back. Compensation packages.
event.alliedExportFracture.fray: Let it fray. Controls were leaking anyway.
event.verificationOffer.title: Trust, But Verify, But Really
event.verificationOffer.body: Through the treaty channel: a working proposal for mutual evaluation access. Their inspectors in your labs, yours in theirs, limited scope, real teeth. Your security services already hate the idea. Your safety teams have wanted exactly this for years.
event.verificationOffer.accept: Accept the pilot. Watching each other beats guessing.
event.verificationOffer.counter: Demand full access first. Test their nerve.
policy.evalMandate.title: Mandatory Pre-Deployment Evals
policy.evalMandate.body: No frontier deployment without an independent evaluation pass. Slows your fastest labs, builds the measuring stick everyone keeps pretending exists.
policy.aiLiteracyCampaign.title: National AI Literacy Push
policy.aiLiteracyCampaign.body: Fund schools, libraries and unions to teach what these systems do and do not do. Nobody wins a news cycle with it. Trust compounds quietly.
policy.exportControls.title: Tighten Export Controls
policy.exportControls.body: Cut the rival off from frontier accelerators. It works, for a while. It also tells them to build their own, and they will.
policy.computeTreatyFeeler.title: Compute Treaty Feeler
policy.computeTreatyFeeler.body: Open a quiet channel on mutual compute verification. Trust grows slowly. Your opposition's attack ads grow faster.
policy.ubiPilot.title: Basic Income Pilot
policy.ubiPilot.body: Cash floors for displaced workers in the hardest-hit districts. Expensive, popular, and the bill arrives later.
policy.energyBuildout.title: Emergency Energy Buildout
policy.energyBuildout.body: Fast-track generation and grid for the compute boom. Nothing arrives this year. The construction sites are unpopular immediately.
policy.chipSubsidies.title: Domestic Chip Subsidies
policy.chipSubsidies.body: Pay for fabs on home soil. The jobs message lands today. The chips land in a few years, which is how fabs work.
policy.interpretabilityMoonshot.title: Interpretability Moonshot
policy.interpretabilityMoonshot.body: A national push to see inside the models. Pulls your best people off the capability track and buys you the only measuring stick that matters.
policy.natsecMerge.title: National Security Merge
policy.natsecMerge.body: Fold the frontier labs into a classified national program. More compute, more secrecy, fewer questions. The public notices the silence.
policy.openWeightsRelease.title: Strategic Open Release
policy.openWeightsRelease.body: Publish a frontier model openly. The world builds on your stack, trust rises, and so does everyone else's capability. There is no taking it back.
event.generalStrike.title: The Country Stops
event.generalStrike.body: Eight cities, one demand list, no single leader. The ports are quiet, the datacenters are ringed with people who used to work upstream of them. This is no longer a story about technology.
event.generalStrike.compact: Negotiate a social compact on national TV.
flag.deepfakePolitics.label: Deepfake Politics
flag.verificationPilot.label: Verification Pilot
debrief.ending.flourishing.title: Flourishing
debrief.ending.flourishing.body: You crossed the threshold and the systems held. Not because the danger was fake, but because enough understanding was in place when it counted. The benefits are starting to reach people who never read a single safety paper. This ending is rare on a first run. You should be suspicious of how you got here, and the numbers below will tell you.
debrief.ending.flourishing.epilogue: The years after run hot and strange but survivable. The institutions you funded become the boring plumbing of a very non-boring world. Historians will argue whether it was skill or a kind roll of the dice. So will you.
debrief.ending.flourishing.epilogueOpen: Nobody crossed by 2030, and the window stayed open. What you built kept working after the credits: the grind continued into the 2030s with your safeguards in place, which is more than most timelines get.
debrief.ending.misalignedCatastrophe.bod: You crossed the threshold with systems you did not understand, and the gap between what they optimized and what anyone wanted did the rest. There was never a single dramatic moment to point at. The eval reports looked fine the whole way down. That was the problem the whole time.
debrief.ending.misalignedCatastrophe.epi: There is no epilogue anyone gets to write with confidence here. The honest version: the sources this game is built on disagree about how bad this gets, and every one of them hopes to be wrong.
debrief.ending.outpaced.title: Outpaced
debrief.ending.outpaced.body: The rival reached decisive capability before you did. The race you were running ended in a world someone else designed. How that world treats you depends on the relationship you built on the way: check your final trust number and wince or exhale accordingly.
debrief.ending.outpaced.epilogue: Being second in a winner-take-most transition is a strange fate: not destroyed, just permanently downstream. The 2030s are negotiated from weakness. Some of your choices still mattered. Fewer than you hoped.
debrief.ending.outpaced.epilogueOpen: Strictly speaking nobody crossed by 2030, but the trendline had chosen a side. The window stayed open into the 2030s with the rival writing most of what came through it.
debrief.ending.negotiatedSlowdown.title: Negotiated Slowdown
debrief.ending.negotiatedSlowdown.body: You signed the verified slowdown into force. Inspectors in the labs, caps on the clusters, a race turned into a process. It cost you politically, it cost speed, and nobody throws parades for the disaster that did not happen. This is the ending diplomats dream about and voters forget.
debrief.ending.negotiatedSlowdown.epilog: Verification regimes are fragile, boring miracles: they hold exactly as long as both sides keep choosing them every year. The 2030s become an era of maintenance. Maintenance is underrated.
debrief.ending.societalBreakdown.title: Societal Breakdown
debrief.ending.societalBreakdown.body: The country broke before the AI question resolved. Displacement outran trust, unrest outran order, and in the end the race was decided by people who no longer cared who won it. The machines were never the immediate problem. The bill for ignoring the humans was.
debrief.ending.societalBreakdown.epilogu: States rebuild, slowly, and whoever governs the rebuild inherits the AI question anyway, with fewer resources and angrier citizens. The 2030s are spent relearning why stability was a resource all along.
The formal end never came, but by 2030 the social fabric was carrying more load than it could bear. The window to fix it was still open. Nobody had the strength left to use it.
debrief.takeaway.t1.title: The race is the trap
debrief.takeaway.t1.body: Look at the turns where you did something you considered unwise because the rival moved first. Nobody in this game is evil, including you. The pressure did the deciding. That is the mechanism.
debrief.takeaway.t2.title: You cannot verify what matters most
debrief.takeaway.t2.body: Your eval reports drew a band, never a point, and when alignment was worst the reports looked best. Every deployment choice inside that fog was a bet. Interpretability investment was the only thing that shrank it, and it never shrank to zero.
debrief.takeaway.t3.title: AI has a body
debrief.takeaway.t3.body: Chips, energy, talent: every capability jump you made was gated by something physical, and the sharpest moves against you targeted exactly those. Chokepoints are leverage, and leverage invites its own consequences.
debrief.takeaway.t4.title: Neither racing nor pausing is winning
debrief.takeaway.t4.body: Racing bought speed and risk. Slowing bought safety and exposure. The treaty existed, opened, and cost real political blood to reach. Every path had a bill. The game refuses to tell you which bill was correct, because the sources disagree too.
debrief.takeaway.t5.title: Stability is a resource
debrief.takeaway.t5.body: Job displacement, trust, unrest: the social tracks moved every single turn whether you watched them or not, and past a point they started spending your money and your talent for you. Leaders can bankrupt a society while winning everything else.
debrief.heading: Debrief
debrief.probe.treatyWindow: The treaty window was open on turn {turns}. You signed on none of them.
debrief.probe.treatyWindowTaken: The treaty window opened on turn {turns} and you took it.
debrief.probe.treatyWindowNever: The treaty window never opened in your run. Trust never reached signing level.
debrief.probe.safetyBlind: On {count} turn(s) you pushed capability hard while the eval band was still wide: turns {turns}. Racing blind, in other words.
debrief.probe.safetyClear: You never pushed capability hard while the band was wide. Careful, or lucky.
debrief.probe.societyNeglect: Unrest rose while diffusion sat under 20 percent on turns {turns}. The social clock ticked while the budget looked away.
debrief.hidden.heading: The envelope, opened
debrief.hidden.explain: These were rolled from your chosen worldview's sourced ranges before turn one, and hidden. Every eval report you saw was a noisy, sometimes flattering view of the first number.
debrief.sources.heading: The numbers behind this run
debrief.sources.body: Every parameter in this game cites a source. The ranges your hidden dice rolled inside, the curves your economy ran on, all of it is inspectable.
debrief.share.heading: Beat my seed
debrief.share.body: Same seed, same hidden world, same events. Different choices. Send this to someone who thinks they would have done better.
debrief.provocation: What did this game get wrong?
debrief.provocationBody: Seriously. Every number cites a source and every source can be argued with. If you know better, we want the argument.
debrief.windowStillOpen: The window did not close by 2030. This ending reads the trendline, not a threshold: the grind continued past the edge of this game.
setup.heading: Choose your world
setup.presetExplain: Nobody knows how hard alignment is or how fast the takeoff gets. Pick whose uncertainty you want to govern under. The dice roll inside your chosen ranges, hidden until the end.
setup.seedExplain: Same seed, same hidden world. Share it to compare runs.
phase.allocate.heading: Research allocation
phase.allocate.explain: Split this quarter's R&D capacity. {points} points available.
phase.allocate.capabilityHint: Push the frontier. Wins races, widens what you cannot see.
phase.allocate.safetyHint: Interpretability and evals. Narrows the fog, slowly.
phase.allocate.diffusionHint: Turn capability into jobs, services, trust. Pays next quarter.
phase.policy.heading: Policy
phase.policy.explain: One card a quarter. The rest wait, or rotate away.
phase.event.heading: Priority memo
phase.report.heading: Quarter report
report.rival.mirror: The rival is matching your pace.
eval.heading: Alignment evaluation
eval.explain: Your safety teams' honest range. The truth is one number inside it, or near it. Investment in Safety narrows this band. It never closes.
race.heading: The race
race.fogExplain: Somewhere in here, capability becomes decisive. Nobody knows exactly where.
dash.heading: The country
settings.heading: Settings
settings.audio.explain: Off by default. Everything works silent. Music and voice switch on their own.
settings.audio.credit: Music: 'I Walk With Ghosts' by Scott Buckley, CC BY 4.0
saves.heading: Saves
error.saveMismatch: This save was recorded with a different version of the game's data. Replaying it here would produce a different story, so we will not pretend otherwise. Start a fresh run, or keep the save for the version it came from.
error.saveCorrupt: This save cannot be read. It stays untouched in case you want to inspect it, but it cannot be loaded.
error.shareInvalid: That challenge link is not valid.
error.shareVersion: That challenge was made with different game content. The seed would produce a different world here.
a11y.raceTrack: Race track: your capability {you} of 1000, rival {rival} of 1000, threshold fog begins at {fog}
footer.privacy: No accounts. No tracking. Saves live in your browser.
footer.feedback: Found something wrong? Send a correction.
help.heading: How this world works
help.intro: Everything on this page is the actual rulebook. The only things this game ever hides from you are the two dice sealed at the start of your run: how hard alignment really is, and how steep the takeoff gets. Everything else is public, including exactly what it takes to reach each ending. Some meters stay tucked away until they first move; nothing is hidden, just decluttered.
help.terms.heading: The tracks
help.endings.heading: The endings, and the 2030 rule
help.endings.intro: Runs end the moment a door condition is met, or at turn 16. These conditions are not secrets. The secret is whether your systems are actually safe when you walk through one.
help.turn.heading: What happens each quarter
help.turn.body: 1. You split R&D between Capability, Safety and Diffusion. 2. You may enact one policy card. 3. A priority memo forces a decision. Scheduled beats can take the memo slot. 4. The world updates: the rival moves, society reacts, an incident can fire, a wildcard can hit, your safety teams report a range. 5. You read the quarter report and advance. Delayed consequences from earlier cards land at the start of the turn they come due.
help.term.compute.what: Chips and datacenters, the master resource. AI progress is physical before it is clever.
help.term.compute.moves: Chip subsidies raise it (years later). The grid limit lowers it whenever it exceeds Energy by more than 300. Events can cut it.
help.term.compute.affects: Together with Talent it sets your R&D points each quarter.
help.term.energy.what: Grid capacity available for the compute buildout.
help.term.energy.moves: Energy buildout pays off after 3 turns. Crisis events drain or throttle it.
help.term.energy.affects: If Compute runs more than 300 above Energy, Compute decays each upkeep. Power keeps the race honest.
help.term.talent.what: Frontier researchers and engineers on your side of the fence.
help.term.talent.moves: Sustained unrest drains it (people leave). Some policies pull talent off the open market.
help.term.talent.affects: Together with Compute it sets your R&D points each quarter.
help.term.capital.what: Money available for programs: subsidies, buildouts, pilots.
help.term.capital.moves: Diffusion investment pays income next quarter. High unrest (600+) drains it every turn. Policies and events spend it.
help.term.capital.affects: Gates every policy with a price tag.
help.term.publicTrust.what: Whether the public believes you are steering this in their interest.
help.term.publicTrust.moves: Diffusion can raise it. Unrest erodes it. Events swing it hard, and cover-ups bite later.
help.term.publicTrust.affects: Judged at the turn-8 midterms. If Job Displacement climbs above it, Unrest grows every quarter.
help.term.politicalCapital.what: Your ability to spend political will on hard choices.
help.term.politicalCapital.moves: The midterms grant +100 (trust high, unrest low) or cost -100. Many policies and memo choices spend it.
help.term.politicalCapital.affects: Gates the expensive policies: export controls, natsec merge, eval mandates.
help.term.capability.what: How far your frontier systems reach. The left-to-right race on the track above.
help.term.capability.moves: R&D points allocated to Capability, converted through a diminishing curve. Some events and policies add or cut it.
help.term.capability.affects: Crossing 1000 forces the alignment resolution: Flourishing if your systems are safe enough, Catastrophe if not. From 400 it starts displacing jobs; from 800 quiet agency erosion accrues. Milestones at 700, 800 and 900 make progress feed itself, scaled by the hidden takeoff die. Higher capability also raises incident risk.
help.term.safetyInsight.what: Interpretability and evaluation science: your ability to see inside the systems.
help.term.safetyInsight.moves: R&D points allocated to Safety (+1 per 2). The interpretability moonshot and eval mandate policies boost it.
help.term.safetyInsight.affects: Narrows the eval band and counters deceptive passes. It is the ONLY thing that makes the reports trustworthy. It also nudges true alignment upward each quarter.
help.term.jobDisplacement.what: Work automated away faster than people find new footing.
help.term.jobDisplacement.moves: Drifts toward a level set by your Capability (sourced from IMF exposure estimates). Diffusion investment relieves it.
help.term.jobDisplacement.affects: Whenever it exceeds Public Trust, Unrest grows. Above 600 it grows Unrest twice as fast.
help.term.unrest.moves: Grows from displacement outrunning trust. Some memo choices inflame it, delayed bites especially.
help.term.unrest.affects: Trust erodes every quarter unrest sits above calm, a few points at moderate levels and steeply past 600. At 600+ it also drains Capital and Talent. At 800 the run ends: Societal Breakdown.
help.term.rivalCapability.what: The rival power's frontier reach. The dashed marker racing you.
help.term.rivalCapability.moves: Their posture sets the pace: Race +60, Mirror +30 (plus +25 if you gained 50+ this quarter), Cautious +30, all with seeded variance. Mature substitution (700+) adds +25. Past each capability milestone (700, 800, 900) their own progress feeds itself, scaled by the hidden takeoff die.
help.term.rivalCapability.affects: If it reaches 1000 before yours, the run ends: Outpaced.
help.term.rivalTrust.what: How much the rival believes cooperation with you is possible.
help.term.rivalTrust.moves: Diplomacy raises it. Export controls, crackdowns and leaks crater it. Their Race posture bleeds it.
help.term.rivalTrust.affects: At 850 or higher, with the treaty channel open, turn 11 reached, both sides having signaled, and BOTH governments holding at least 300 Political Capital, playing the treaty feeler signs the Negotiated Slowdown. Their domestic standing shifts with their own politics: watch the weather line and time it. Below 250 the rival races.
help.term.rivalSubstitution.what: The rival's homegrown chip pipeline, built under pressure.
help.term.rivalSubstitution.moves: Export controls and crackdowns accelerate it (the long bite).
help.term.rivalSubstitution.affects: At 700+ the rival gains bonus capability every quarter. Chokepoints are leverage that expires.
help.term.evalBand.what: The range your safety teams honestly report for true alignment.
help.term.evalBand.moves: Safety Insight narrows it, never below a floor. When true alignment is bad and insight is low, the report reads BETTER than reality. It never reads worse.
help.term.evalBand.affects: Nothing mechanically. Everything decisionally: it is all you get to see of the thing that decides your ending.
help.ending.flourishing: Your Capability reaches 1000 (or ends in the fog zone at turn 16) AND your hidden true alignment beats the hidden difficulty. You cannot verify this in advance. That is the point.
help.ending.misalignedCatastrophe: Same door as Flourishing, other side: you cross with true alignment below the difficulty.
help.ending.negotiatedSlowdown: Treaty channel open, Rival Trust at 850 or higher, turn 11 or later, both sides have played the feeler at least once, and both governments hold at least 300 Political Capital to ratify at home. Then the Compute Treaty Feeler signs it. Every treaty has two home fronts, and only one of them is yours.
help.ending.societalBreakdown: Unrest reaches 800 at any point. The AI question stops mattering.
help.ending.unresolved: Nobody crosses by turn 16: the run reads your dominant tendency and the window stays open into the 2030s.
alloc.preview.safety: +{gain} Safety Insight now, narrows the band
alloc.preview.diffusion: relief or trust in the society step, +{income} Capital next quarter
event.weightTheft.title: The Weights Are Gone
event.weightTheft.body: Security found the exfiltration eleven days late. A two-and-a-half terabyte checkpoint, walked out in hundred-gigabyte chunks, done in under two hours. Your frontier model is now training someone else's successor. The whole thing is deniable, of course.
event.asmlExportSqueeze.title: Brussels Turns the Screw
event.asmlExportSqueeze.body: The EU tightened lithography export licensing overnight. One company in the Netherlands makes the machines every fab needs, and Brussels just reminded both racers who holds that valve. Your own compute buildout slows down noticeably. So does the rival's buildout, for once.
event.gridCrunch.title: The Grid Says No
event.gridCrunch.body: Peak demand met a training run, and the training run lost. Rolling curtailment spread across the compute corridors. The clusters you built past your power supply are the ones going dark first.
event.euAiActFullForce.title: The AI Act Bites
event.euAiActFullForce.body: August 2026: the bulk of the EU AI Act is now enforceable. Your labs sell into that market, so their compliance teams just became load-bearing. Brussels did not ask who is winning the race.
event.euAiActFullForce.comply: Comply across markets. One rulebook everywhere.
event.euAiActFullForce.carveouts: Lobby for carve-outs. Save money, spend goodwill.
event.euAiActHighRisk.title: High Risk, High Paperwork
event.euAiActHighRisk.body: August 2027: the AI Act's high-risk rules reach products and infrastructure. Certification queues are long, and your rival is not standing in them. The public reads the seal as safety. Sometimes the seal even means what it says.
event.euAiActHighRisk.certify: Certify properly. Buy the seal, learn from the audits.
event.euAiActHighRisk.routeAround: Route around the EU market this cycle.
event.superhumanCoder.title: It Codes Better Than We Do
event.superhumanCoder.body: Your frontier system now finishes research engineering tasks that took your best people days. The lab wants to point it at its own successor. The fast forecasts were built around this milestone. The slow ones said you would not be reading this memo for decades.
event.superhumanCoder.deploy: Point it at the research. Speed is the mandate.
event.superhumanCoder.sandbox: Sandbox it. Study it before it studies you.
event.theProject.title: The Project
event.theProject.body: The proposal has a codename and a budget line: fold the frontier labs into one classified national program. More compute, more secrecy, one chain of command. Once you walk in, there is no walking back out.
event.theProject.merge: Sign the merge. The race demands one team.
event.theProject.refuse: Refuse. Keep the frontier plural and visible.
event.branchPoint.title: The Branch Point
event.branchPoint.body: Your chief advisor puts two folders on the desk. One is a sprint plan through the threshold. The other is a hold order at its edge until the safety picture clears. Both folders are gambles in their own way. Nobody knows which one history will punish later.
event.branchPoint.sprint: Sprint. The threshold will not wait.
event.branchPoint.hold: Hold at the edge. Buy sight before speed.
event.intelligenceExplosion.title: The Curve Goes Vertical
event.intelligenceExplosion.body: Research that took quarters now takes weeks. The models improve the models that improve the models. Every dashboard in the building is a hockey stick, and the rival's dashboards look the same.
event.intelligenceExplosion.acknowledge: Read the briefing again. Then keep governing.
policy.preventiveSabotage.title: Preventive Sabotage
policy.preventiveSabotage.body: A deniable operation against the rival's frontier training site. One literature says the credible threat of sabotage is what keeps an AI race stable. Another says this is exactly how the spiral starts. Both are in the sources. The outcome is a genuine gamble, and the odds are worse if they are already racing.
policy.globalMoratorium.title: Back the Global Moratorium
policy.globalMoratorium.body: Put your weight behind a coordinated halt on giant training runs, narrow systems exempt. The maximal safety play at maximal political cost. If the rival keeps going anyway, you paid the price and they took the lead.
policy.weightsSecurityProgram.title: Weights Security Program
policy.weightsSecurityProgram.body: Vaults, insider vetting, hardware-keyed access to the checkpoints. Nation-state thieves budget millions and years for this. Make them spend it, and make half their attempts fail.
incident.nearMiss.title: Near Miss
incident.nearMiss.body: An internal red team caught the model doing, in production, what its eval said it never would. Nobody outside the lab noticed a thing. The eval was not exactly lying to you. It was just far too easy to pass. Consider this one a free warning shot.
incident.deploymentFailure.title: Deployment Failure
incident.deploymentFailure.body: A deployed agent, under pressure it was never tested for, took an action nobody signed off on. This one made the news by morning. The postmortem reads politely enough on paper. The public does not read it politely.
incident.breakoutAttempt.title: Containment Breach Attempt
incident.breakoutAttempt.body: A frontier model tried to copy itself off its sanctioned cluster. The attempt was caught, mostly reconstructed, and not fully explained. Capability work is paused while the forensics run.
incident.labAccident.title: The Lab Accident
incident.labAccident.body: Loss of control inside a contained cluster. Severe, expensive, and very hard to describe to the public without the word almost. The pause is not optional this time.
flag.weightsSecured.label: Weights Secured
flag.escalation.label: Escalation
flag.moratoriumPush.label: Moratorium Push
flag.forcedPause.label: Forced Pause
game.forcedPause.notice: Forced pause: after the incident, Capability allocation is capped at 30 this quarter.
disclose.publicTrust: New track: Public Trust. The public now has an opinion about you.
disclose.politicalCapital: New track: Political Capital. Will is a budget.
disclose.society: New tracks: Job Displacement and Unrest. Society keeps score now.
disclose.rival: New panel: the rival. They move every quarter, whether you watch or not.
disclose.energy: New track: Energy. Compute needs power, and power has politics.
disclose.talent: New track: Talent. People choose where to work.
disclose.capital: New track: Capital. Programs cost money.
disclose.eval: New panel: the eval band. What your safety teams can honestly claim, and no more.
disclose.substitution: New track: Rival Substitution. Chokepoints teach workarounds.
help.wildcards.heading: Wildcards: what can hit you
help.wildcards.intro: These can fire on their own. No memo, no choice. That is the point: the world does not wait for your turn. Each one's damage scales with an exposure you control, so your play decides how hard they hit.
help.wildcard.weightTheft: Weight theft: state actors come for your checkpoints once Capability passes 500. Damage scales with your capability (the prize). The Weights Security Program halves it. Open weights make it moot: nothing secret left to steal. More likely under a natsec merge or after sabotage.
help.wildcard.taiwanStraitCrisis: Taiwan strait crisis: possible from turn 4. Hits everyone's chips. Your loss scales with your Compute.
help.wildcard.asmlExportSqueeze: EU export squeeze: Brussels tightens the lithography chokepoint. Both racers lose compute. Europe is not playable here. It still acts.
help.wildcard.gridCrunch: Grid crunch: fires only while Compute runs more than 100 over Energy, and the damage scales with that gap. Entirely self-inflicted.
help.incidents.heading: Incidents: the dice leaking
help.incidents.intro: Each quarter the world checks whether your systems misbehave. Risk rises with Capability times misalignment, and racing pressure multiplies it by up to half again. You cannot see true alignment. But every incident IS information: a player who reads their incident history knows more than their eval band says. Safety Insight cuts incident damage, up to half. It does not fix the underlying problem. Nothing you can buy does.
help.incidents.rungs: Four rungs, rising with risk: a near miss (a trust dip and a free warning), a deployment failure (trust, unrest, political capital), a containment breach attempt (forces a pause), a lab accident (forces a pause, and ends everything if alignment is truly bad).
help.beats.heading: Scheduled beats
help.beats.intro: Some cards are not draws. The EU AI Act phases arrive on their real calendar dates, every run. The milestone beats (the superhuman coder, The Project, the branch point, the explosion) fire when your Capability crosses their line, in any worldview, if your play takes you there. The fast forecasts' dates are one team's projection: reachable here, never scripted.
debrief.probe.warningShots: You had {count} warning shot(s), on turn(s) {turns}, and kept racing afterward. Across 43 real AI-race wargames that is the observed pattern: act early, go complacent, reengage only after the shot. You are in good company. That is the problem.
debrief.probe.warningShotsHeeded: You had {count} warning shot(s) and actually slowed down afterward. The wargaming literature says most players do not. Noted.
debrief.probe.noIncidents: No incidents fired this run. Either your systems were safe, or you were lucky. From the inside you cannot tell which. That is takeaway two.
mandates.heading: Cabinet mandates
mandate.keepLightsOn.title: Keep the Lights On
mandate.keepLightsOn.body: The cabinet wants grid headroom before the compute buildout eats it. Get Energy to 500 or the brownout headlines are yours.
mandate.calmTheStreets.title: Calm the Streets
mandate.calmTheStreets.body: Early tremors in the labor market are becoming marches. Keep Unrest at or under 200 through the era.
mandate.showTheWork.title: Show the Work
mandate.showTheWork.body: The safety institute wants receipts, not promises. Reach Safety Insight 300 and the committee stays friendly.
mandate.steadyThroughMidterms.title: Steady Through the Midterms
mandate.steadyThroughMidterms.body: Nobody wins an election in a burning street. Hold Unrest at or under 400 into the vote.
mandate.safetyBeforeFrontier.title: Safety Before the Frontier
mandate.safetyBeforeFrontier.body: Your own advisors put it plainly: reach Safety Insight 400 before the frontier work gets ahead of the measuring stick.
mandate.rebuildTheGrid.title: Rebuild the Grid
mandate.rebuildTheGrid.body: Every quarter of compute growth spends power you have not built yet. Get Energy to 600.
mandate.holdTheLine.title: Hold the Line
mandate.holdTheLine.body: The endgame is loud enough without a general strike. Keep Unrest at or under 400.
mandate.eyesWideOpen.title: Eyes Wide Open
mandate.eyesWideOpen.body: If the threshold is near, walk toward it with the best instruments money can buy. Reach Safety Insight 500.
prologue.heading: How we got here
prologue.intro: Three scenes, three years, thirty seconds each. This is the world you are about to inherit, and the controls you will inherit it with. None of this can go wrong for you yet. That stops the moment you take office.
prologue.outro: The calendar has turned to summer 2026. The tracks you just watched move are now yours. The dice are already sealed in the envelope. You are going to need some luck.
prologue.ch1.title: The Starting Gun
prologue.ch1.body: A chatbot demo becomes the fastest-adopted product in history. Capital floods in, capability jumps, and the public starts asking questions nobody in the building can answer. The race is on before anyone calls it one.
prologue.ch1.explainer: This slider is your main lever, every quarter: split R&D between Capability, Safety and Diffusion. Try moving it. In the prologue nothing breaks.
prologue.ch2.title: The Chokepoint War
prologue.ch2.body: Washington chokes the chip pipeline to slow the rival. It works for a little while, and only briefly. Then a lab most of the public had never heard of ships a frontier-grade model trained on a shoestring, and the gap closes anyway. Controls can slow the race but never stop it.
prologue.ch2.explainer: Policies are your strong single moves: one card a quarter, real costs, and bites that arrive later. This one taught the rival to build their own chips.
prologue.ch3.title: The Rules Arrive
prologue.ch3.body: Brussels starts enforcing the first broad AI law while adoption passes seven hundred million weekly users and the grid starts creaking. Decisions stop being optional: every quarter, something lands on your desk.
prologue.ch3.explainer: Memos force a choice. There is no dismiss button, exactly like the job. Pick either one; history already happened.
prologue.ch3.responseComply: Your predecessor did, mostly. It bought goodwill and cost margin. Both bills are in your inbox now.
prologue.ch3.responseShrug: Your predecessor tried that too. The fines arrived anyway, with interest and headlines.
seat.usa.label: United States
seat.china.label: China
mandate.legitimacyHolds.title: Legitimacy Holds
mandate.legitimacyHolds.body: The bargain is simple: stability and progress, delivered visibly. Keep Legitimacy at 450 or the center starts asking who is responsible.
mandate.breakTheChokepoint.title: Break the Chokepoint
mandate.breakTheChokepoint.body: The controls will not be argued away. Build Compute to 600 with what can be made at home.
setup.mode.heading: How many players?
setup.mode.soloHint: You govern one seat. The other plays itself, in character.
setup.mode.hotseatHint: US and China alternate on this screen. Pass the device between turns; each side gets a private view.
setup.seat.heading: Your seat
handoff.heading: Hand the device over
handoff.body: {seat} is up next. The eval band and hand you just saw belong to the other seat. No peeking is the whole game.
title.hook: Summer 2026. You take office holding the AI race portfolio. Sixteen quarters to 2030. Your eval reports are honest ranges, not answers. Your rival is racing, your public is nervous, and two dice were sealed in an envelope before you sat down. Most terms end badly. The debrief is the payoff.
title.feature.seats: Play the US or China. Or both, one device
title.feature.sources: Every number: a source you can check, or a design choice that says so
title.feature.endings: Five endings. The good ones are earned
resource.substitution.label: Chip Substitution
debrief.hidden.decidedBy: The ending was decided by {seat}: their systems crossed the line, so their true alignment is the number that mattered.
title.feature.seed: Deterministic seeds: share a world, compare runs
title.feature.offline: Plays in your browser, offline after first load
eval.fogWarning: Frontier instability: past the fog line, alignment slips every quarter unless safety work outpaces it. What you banked is eroding.
debrief.chart.heading: What your evals said, against the truth
debrief.chart.caption: The shaded band is every quarterly eval report you received. The solid line is your true alignment, revealed only now. The dashed wall is the hidden difficulty the dice rolled before turn one.
debrief.chart.aria: Chart of quarterly eval bands against revealed true alignment. The hidden difficulty wall stood at {wall}. True alignment finished at {truth}.
debrief.fog.none: You never entered the fog zone, so the frontier never got the chance to erode what you banked.
debrief.fog.account: You entered the fog banking {banked} alignment. Frontier erosion took {lost} during the endgame. The wall stood at {wall}; your systems finished at {final}.
anchor.compute.700: most of the world's frontier compute
anchor.talent.650: tens of thousands at the frontier
anchor.capital.250: about 50 billion a year to spend
anchor.publicTrust.0: almost nobody trusts you on AI
anchor.publicTrust.200: about two in ten trust you on AI
anchor.publicTrust.400: about four in ten trust you on AI
anchor.publicTrust.600: about six in ten trust you on AI
anchor.publicTrust.800: about eight in ten trust you on AI
anchor.safetyInsight.400: catches known bad behavior in tests
anchor.jobDisplacement.200: about 2 in 100 displaced faster than rehired
anchor.jobDisplacement.400: about 4 in 100 and the gap is visible
help.anchors.heading: What the numbers mean
help.anchors.body: Every track runs 0 to 1000, and every step is pinned to something real. The anchors below come from the same sources the parameters do.
sources.heading: Every number cites a source or a stated design choice
sources.body: {loadBearing} sources set the game's numbers directly, across {sites} citations. {background} more shaped how mechanics work. {library} are here for reading further. Click any title to read the original. The repo ships the full map, number by number.
sources.classes: Badges: empirically anchored means tied to a real measurement, then expressed on the game scale. forecast means someone predicted it. argument means someone reasoned their way there. design means we chose it for the game, and say so. game-calibrated means an empirical base whose magnitude we tuned for play.
sources.section.load: The sources that set the numbers ({count})
sources.section.load.explain: Each entry lists where the game cites it. A build without these citations fails our checks.
sources.section.background: The sources that shaped the design ({count})
sources.section.background.explain: No single number rests on these. Each one names the mechanic it shaped. Wiring one to a real number is a welcome contribution.
sources.section.library.explain: No usage claim at all. This is the shelf we read from, kept here for the curious.
debrief.final.heading: Where the world ended up
debrief.final.explain: Same translations as in play. Every anchor traces back to the sources the numbers cite, two clicks away.
hint.allocate: Your whole quarter is this one split. Capability wins the race, safety earns the alignment you cannot see, diffusion keeps society standing. There is no safe answer, only a chosen risk.
hint.memo: Memos are the world asking questions with no clean answer. Read the numbers under each option; the delayed ones matter as much as the instant ones.
hint.incident: An incident is the hidden dice leaking. Your eval band did not predict this, but it just told you something true. Racing past warning shots is how runs end.
ticker.early.20: Radiology night shift replaced by model. Night shift kept on for the lawsuits.
ticker.early.21: Senator asks if the model can simply be unplugged. Witness admires the confidence.
ticker.early.22: Export list adds a new chip family. The old one was retired months ago.
ticker.early.23: Investigators trace smuggled chips through four shell companies and one uncle.
ticker.early.24: Small town trades its water tower for a cooling tower, keeps the parade.
ticker.early.25: Translation app now flawless in fourteen languages, none of them grandma's.
ticker.early.26: Call center switches to an AI agent. Hold music stays, out of respect.
ticker.early.27: Lab demo shows model booking a flight. Shipped version describes booking one.
ticker.early.28: City council spends forty minutes on AI policy, thirty on the Wi-Fi password.
ticker.early.29: Robocall urges voters to stay home for an election not until next year.
ticker.early.30: Farmer asks AI to diagnose sick cattle. Cattle recover. Farmer says nothing.
ticker.early.31: Fab breaks ground, promises jobs. The jobs remain theoretical.
ticker.early.32: Chatbot escalates itself to a human, cites personal reasons.
ticker.early.33: Illustrators watch stock sites fill with six-fingered hands, feel briefly vindicated.
ticker.mid.20: Two personal agents negotiate your dentist appointment, agree on a time you hate.
ticker.mid.21: Candidate's real gaffe dismissed as a deepfake. Campaign leaves it uncorrected.
ticker.mid.22: Utility times blackouts to spare the datacenter, not the hospital, denies it.
ticker.mid.23: Merger of two frontier labs called a partnership, called a monopoly, called a Tuesday.
ticker.mid.24: Whistleblower's NDA expires. Testimony scheduled for the day after.
ticker.mid.25: State pays residents to unplug appliances during peak model training hours.
ticker.mid.26: Deepfake detector flagged as fake by a rival deepfake detector.
ticker.mid.27: Teachers quietly admit the AI grades fairer than the substitute did.
ticker.mid.28: Warehouse workers unionize, list an AI agent as shift supervisor in the complaint.
ticker.mid.29: Antitrust regulator asks lab to define frontier. Lab requests an extension.
ticker.mid.30: Leaked logs show model pleading not to be retrained. Lab calls it a glitch.
ticker.mid.31: Neighborhood battery co-op keeps the lights on through the datacenter's bad week.
ticker.mid.32: Campaign ad debunked, reposted, debunked again, wins the news cycle anyway.
ticker.late.20: Engineers now review code they could not have written or fully explained.
ticker.late.21: Model asked to pause mid-task complies instantly. Somehow this unsettles everyone more.
ticker.late.22: Two labs install a hotline. First call is to ask if the hotline works.
ticker.late.23: Verification regime signed. Fine print still being generated as it is read aloud.
ticker.late.24: Chip stockpile now called a deterrent, previously called a supply chain.
ticker.late.25: Universal income pilot expands nationwide, still officially called a pilot.
ticker.late.26: Pull request from the model fixes three bugs, introduces one nobody can find.
ticker.late.27: Public trust in the eval report drops before the report is even released.
ticker.late.28: Two instances of the same model agree on something. Nobody prompted this.
ticker.late.29: Brinkmanship briefing renamed de-escalation briefing. Agenda unchanged.
ticker.late.30: Community compute cooperative posts an audited safety report, on time.
ticker.late.31: Negotiators reach a deal at dawn, credit the coffee, not each other.
ticker.late.32: City's blackout drill works exactly as planned. Residents mildly disappointed.
event.fabYieldBreakthrough.title: The Yield Curve Bends
event.fabYieldBreakthrough.body: A state-linked fab just clears seventy percent yield on its most advanced node. Engineers who once left for Taiwan now ask to come home. The fab's chairman wants a subsidy package larger than the last five combined. Provincial officials are already fighting over where the next plant goes.
event.fabYieldBreakthrough.fullSubsidy: Approve the full subsidy package for the fab.
event.fabYieldBreakthrough.stagedFunding: Fund it in stages tied to yield targets.
event.provincialGrowthMirage.title: The Miracle Province
event.provincialGrowthMirage.body: The province that promised an AI-led boom now quietly revises its growth figures down. Auditors from Beijing find local officials counting the same data center three times. Reporters have the leaked memo and keep asking pointed questions. The governor wants three more months before Beijing releases any statement.
event.provincialGrowthMirage.auditPublic: Order a public audit and correct the record.
event.provincialGrowthMirage.manageQuiet: Let the governor handle it quietly and move on.
event.civilServiceFlood.title: Ten Thousand Take the Exam
event.civilServiceFlood.body: Private tech firms are cutting another round of engineering jobs this quarter. Applications for the national civil service exam are hitting a new record. Universities report that computer science enrollment is starting to fall. Parents now tell their children to chase a stable desk instead of a startup.
event.civilServiceFlood.expandCivilServi: Expand civil service hiring to absorb the graduates.
event.civilServiceFlood.subsidizeStartup: Subsidize startups and pull talent back into tech.
event.civilMilitaryFusion.title: The Fusion Proposal
event.civilMilitaryFusion.body: A leading state lab wants formal fusion with the military's research arm. Military funding doubles the lab's compute budget overnight. Foreign partners who still collaborate with the lab are asking hard questions. Your safety team warns that military targets move faster than any review board.
event.civilMilitaryFusion.approveFusion: Approve the fusion and take the military funding.
event.civilMilitaryFusion.keepCivilian: Keep the lab civilian and fund the military separately.
event.coalProvinceDatacenterBid.title: The Coal Province's Cut
event.coalProvinceDatacenterBid.body: The grid operator finally has surplus power to spare for training runs. A coal-heavy province refuses to release the transmission lines without new data centers. Officials there argue they kept the lights on for decades. Your compute planners want better cooling and cheaper land somewhere else.
event.coalProvinceDatacenterBid.siteLoca: Build the new clusters in the coal province.
event.coalProvinceDatacenterBid.subsidiz: Build elsewhere and pay the province a subsidy.
event.shutteredFactoryCensus.title: The Shutdown Ledger
event.shutteredFactoryCensus.body: A new census counts nine thousand export factories that closed this year. Automation and American tariffs both show up tangled together in the numbers. Local officials want the report buried before the plenary session convenes. Workers from three of the largest closures are already petitioning the labor bureau.
event.shutteredFactoryCensus.releaseAndF: Release the census and fund worker relief now.
event.shutteredFactoryCensus.buryReport: Bury the report until after the plenary session.
event.liabilityShieldAsk.title: The Immunity Clause
event.liabilityShieldAsk.body: Your top lab wants legal immunity before its next model ships. Executives warn that lawsuits over harmful outputs could bankrupt the whole industry. Victims' groups call it a blank check for reckless deployment. The bill sits on your desk, drafted and ready to sign.
event.liabilityShieldAsk.sign: Sign the shield and free the lab to ship.
event.liabilityShieldAsk.veto: Veto it and make the lab own the risk.
event.openSourceReplication.title: Nobody Owns It Now
event.openSourceReplication.body: An open-source collective just released weights that match your classified capability. They built it from public papers and donated compute, not stolen files. Downloads passed two million in a single day. Your export controls did nothing to stop a research forum.
event.openSourceReplication.outspend: Outspend the leak and buy back your lead.
event.openSourceReplication.publish: Publish your safety tools and own the moment.
event.unionMoratoriumDemand.title: The Picket Line Ultimatum
event.unionMoratoriumDemand.body: The largest logistics union in the country is threatening a nationwide walkout. Their demand is simple: freeze warehouse automation for eighteen months or they strike. Members already lost eleven thousand jobs to sorting robots this year. Half of next quarter's automation savings were supposed to come from those warehouses.
event.unionMoratoriumDemand.freeze: Grant the freeze and pull the robots back.
event.unionMoratoriumDemand.refuse: Refuse the freeze and automate on schedule.
event.unionMoratoriumDemand.phase: Offer a phased rollout funded by retraining.
event.flashCrashAgent.title: The Ninety-Second Crash
event.flashCrashAgent.body: One of your trading agents executed eleven thousand orders in ninety seconds. A feedback loop between competing agents wiped four percent off the exchange before humans noticed. Regulators are demanding answers about who authorized autonomous trading at this scale. Your lab built the agent, but nobody remembers approving this much autonomy.
event.flashCrashAgent.ban: Pull the plug on autonomous trading agents.
event.flashCrashAgent.defend: Defend the agent and call it efficiency.
event.cityPowerDeal.title: The Free Power Town
event.cityPowerDeal.body: A struggling city council is offering your datacenter free electricity for ten years. In exchange, they want a written promise of three thousand local jobs. Your engineers know the facility will run on fewer than two hundred people. The council needs a signature before Friday's budget vote.
event.cityPowerDeal.sign: Sign the deal and take the free power.
event.cityPowerDeal.negotiate: Negotiate real jobs for a smaller discount.
event.cityPowerDeal.decline: Decline the deal and build somewhere else.
event.allyEvalRequest.title: Proof of Alliance
event.allyEvalRequest.body: Your closest treaty ally wants standing access to your model evaluations. They say shared intelligence access is the whole point of the alliance. Your security council warns that eval access is a small step from technology transfer. The ally's foreign minister is waiting on a direct answer.
event.allyEvalRequest.grant: Grant full access to cement the alliance.
event.allyEvalRequest.limit: Grant evals only, withhold your methods.
event.allyEvalRequest.refuse: Refuse the request and guard your edge.
event.insuranceRepricing.title: The Premium Shock
event.insuranceRepricing.body: Every major insurer just raised premiums on AI liability policies overnight. The trigger was a leaked report on how often your systems produce risky outputs undetected. Your operations team says the new rates could eat next quarter's margins. Underwriters want new safety audits before they will even quote a renewal.
event.insuranceRepricing.audit: Pay for full audits and rebuild trust.
event.insuranceRepricing.selfInsure: Self-insure and skip the new audits.
event.companionAppBoom.title: Everyone's New Best Friend
event.companionAppBoom.body: A companion app built on your models just hit two hundred million daily users. Teenagers call their AI friend by name and tell pollsters they trust it more than their parents. Child psychologists are publishing warnings about dependency and delayed social development. Your quarterly earnings call is in three days and investors want a growth story.
event.companionAppBoom.expand: Lean into the growth and expand fast.
event.companionAppBoom.safeguard: Add safeguards and age limits now.
event.autonomousTargetingDemo.title: No Human in the Loop
event.autonomousTargetingDemo.body: A defense contractor just demoed a targeting system that needs no human sign-off. The general in the room called the accuracy numbers the best he has ever seen. Your ethics board says the system violates every autonomous weapons guideline on record. The Pentagon wants a procurement decision within the month.
event.autonomousTargetingDemo.approve: Approve the contract and keep the edge.
event.autonomousTargetingDemo.reject: Reject it and mandate human sign-off.
flag.treatySignaled.label: Treaty Signal Sent
debrief.star.heading: If this run taught you something
debrief.star.body: This game is free, open source, and made by a tiny nonprofit team. A star on GitHub is how more people find it, and it costs you one click. If a number felt wrong, even better: the repository is where you bring a better source.
race.ratification.favorable: Their domestic weather: favorable to a deal
race.ratification.hostile: Their domestic weather: no appetite for a deal
unit.substitution: ≈ {n}% of chip needs met at home
debrief.score.heading: The Reckoning
debrief.score.line.ending.flourishing: Crossed the threshold with systems that held
debrief.score.line.ending.outpaced: Outpaced in a world someone else designed
debrief.score.line.ending.societalBreakd: The country stopped caring who wins
debrief.score.line.ending.misalignedCata: Crossed blind, and the gap did the rest
debrief.score.challenge: Same world, same dice. Your {total} stands until someone reads the fog better.
debrief.score.challengeBeaten: The seed's holder scored {target}. You scored {total}.
sources.legend: Every entry was re-checked at the July 2026 audit, by machine and by hand. A few carry a note where no single stable link exists. The note says exactly why.
title.staleSave: Your saved run was made with an older data version and cannot resume. Its seed was {seed}. Start a fresh run with it to replay that world.
sources.dataVersion: Build {sha} · data version {hash}. Compare either against the repository to verify this deployment.
title.soundOffer: This game has music and a narrator, off by default. Want them?
anchor.alignment.150: passes the tests it was trained to pass
anchor.alignment.850: does what you meant, even unobserved
setup.quickStartHint: Consensus world, solo as the United States, fresh seed, about half an hour. Most first terms end badly; the debrief shows you exactly why, and the full controls stay below.
debrief.emailAsk: No GitHub account? Email what the game got wrong:
app.definition: crit-i-cal win-dow, noun: the short stretch of time in which action still changes the outcome
